name: Atlantic Jewelry & Loan
description_short: Atlantic Jewelry & Loan is a Denver pawn shop offering collateral-based loans, jewelry sales, repairs, and secondary services like check cashing and computer sales since 1991.
description_long:
Atlantic Jewelry & Loan has operated in Denver since 1991 (per Facebook) or 1998 (per D&B listing—sources conflict), establishing itself as a traditional pawn and jewelry business in the LoDo/downtown corridor. The shop occupies a street-level location at 913 15th Street in Denver's 80202 zip code and maintains regular business hours Monday through Saturday. The company positions itself primarily as a pawnbroker specializing in jewelry transactions and collateral-based lending, a core function typical of the pawn shop category.

Their stated service menu includes pawn loans against jewelry and other collateral, jewelry buy/sell/trade transactions, on-site jewelry repair, check cashing, money orders, and a secondary business purchasing and reselling used laptops. They employ an on-site computer expert to support the electronics sideline. Payment methods accepted include Visa, MasterCard, and Discover, suggesting they handle both cash and card transactions. The breadth of services—from traditional pawning to ancillary financial services like check cashing—reflects a hybrid model common among independent pawn shops seeking revenue diversification.

What distinguishes Atlantic is their explicit investment in on-site expertise: both a dedicated jewelry repair technician and computer specialist suggest they attempt to add value beyond simple collateral assessment and lending. The computer business line is unusual for traditional pawn shops and indicates an attempt to capture secondary-market demand. However, their online presence is minimal (a basic YellowBot listing as the primary entry point), and their official website domain (atlanticpawnstore.com) does not appear to have modern SEO or digital marketing optimization.

The company operates with a 2.5-star rating across only 3 reviews on the YellowBot listing—a very small sample size providing limited insight into customer satisfaction. The limited review volume and absence of substantive online reputation data make it difficult to assess service quality, loan terms, or fee structures. As a traditional pawn shop, customers should expect standard pawn lending dynamics: rapid cash access against collateral with transparent redemption terms, but also higher effective interest rates than traditional lenders due to the nature of collateral-based lending.
pros: Established business operating since 1991 with local market presence in Denver
On-site jewelry repair reduces customer friction and adds service value
On-site computer expert enables secondary revenue stream from laptop sales
Accepts major credit cards (Visa, MasterCard, Discover) in addition to cash
Offers multiple financial services (check cashing, money orders) beyond pawning
Extended business hours (9am-6pm weekdays, 9am-5:30pm Saturday) for customer access
Buy/sell/trade model provides flexibility beyond pawn-only lending
cons: Only 3 reviews with 2.5-star average rating—insufficient data on service quality or customer satisfaction
Conflicting founding date information (1991 vs. 1998) creates credibility uncertainty
Minimal digital presence and outdated website infrastructure reduce transparency on loan terms and rates
No published interest rates, fees, or loan terms disclosed on any listing or website
Computer/laptop business appears secondary and niche, limiting relevance to most pawn shop customers
best_for: Denver residents needing immediate cash against jewelry or electronics collateral
Customers with broken jewelry who want on-site repair combined with pawn options
Local sellers of used laptops or computers seeking quick cash conversion
Unbanked or underbanked individuals needing check cashing or money order services
diagnosis: Atlantic Jewelry & Loan is best for Denver residents who need immediate cash against jewelry or electronics and value on-site repair services, but the minimal online review data (only 3 reviews) and lack of published loan terms create significant transparency gaps. Customers should contact directly to confirm current rates, redemption terms, and loan conditions before pledging collateral.
services: Pawn loans against jewelry and collateral
Credit card and cash payment acceptance
